title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs static delivery, http representations, urls, bases, and origins from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned URL and delivery contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.
Keep platform filesystem paths, POSIX publication paths, percent-encoded URL paths, queries, and fragments as distinct types.
Model root, project-subpath, preview, custom-domain, trailing-slash, and relative-link behavior under one explicit base URL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Static Request Model · changed case · Review Static Request Model
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 6685 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Http Evidence for versioned URL and delivery contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Http Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Http Evidence record says versioned URL and delivery contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Http Evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Http Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Http Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 2856 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Http Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Http Evidence through the concrete versioned URL and delivery contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Http Evidence, keep Http Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Http Evidence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Http Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Http Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review publishing team handles case 2856 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Http Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Correct HTML bytes guarantee correct delivery semantics.
